meaning: n.放气,缩小,通货紧缩,物价低廉(尤指成本不降低时的反常情形)
examples: Deflation is beginning to take hold in the clothing industry.
If the BoJ has a plan to boost economic growth and exorcise deflation while avoiding a debt panic, it is clearly not yet ready to share it.
Merrill Lynch analyst David Errington is concerned about the level of price deflation at Coles supermarkets, which he puts at around 2.2 percent.
He made a wisecrack about dealing with deflation, if necessary, by dropping dollar bills from aircraft.
Once Mr. Bernanke assents to the double-dip recession scenario, he will fight deflation by any means necessary.
